children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Assistance made available under grants awarded under this subpart shall be directed to the chief elected official of the city or urban county that administers the public health agency that provides outpatient and ambulatory services to the greatest number of individuals with AIDS, as reported to and confirmed by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, in the eligible area that is awarded such a grant.

children.label: I
children.text: the number of AIDS cases in such subdivisions;

children.label: II
children.text: the severity of need for outpatient and ambulatory care services in such subdivisions; and

children.label: III
children.text: the health and support services personnel needs of such subdivisions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: establish an HIV health services planning council in accordance with subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: that provide HIV-related health services; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for which the number of cases reported for purposes of constitutes not less than 10 percent of the number of such cases reported for the eligible area.section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.heading: HIV health services planning council
children.label: b HIV health services planning council

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: To be eligible for assistance under this subpart, the chief elected official described in subsection (a)(1) shall establish or designate an HIV health services planning council that shall reflect in its composition the demographics of the population of individuals with HIV/AIDS in the eligible area involved, with particular consideration given to disproportionately affected and historically underserved groups and subpopulations. Nominations for membership on the council shall be identified through an open process and candidates shall be selected based on locally delineated and publicized criteria. Such criteria shall include a conflict-of-interest standard that is in accordance with paragraph (5).

children.label: A
children.text: health care providers, including federally qualified health centers;

children.label: B
children.text: community-based organizations serving affected populations and AIDS service organizations;

children.label: C
children.text: social service providers, including providers of housing and homeless services;

children.label: D
children.text: mental health and substance abuse providers;

children.label: E
children.text: local public health agencies;

children.label: F
children.text: hospital planning agencies or health care planning agencies;

children.label: G
children.text: affected communities, including people with HIV/AIDS, members of a Federally recognized Indian tribe as represented in the population, individuals co-infected with hepatitis B or C and historically underserved groups and subpopulations;

children.label: H
children.text: nonelected community leaders;

children.label: I
children.text: State government (including the State medicaid agency and the agency administering the program under part B);

children.label: J
children.text: grantees under subpart II of part C;1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: K
children.text: grantees under , or, if none are operating in the area, representatives of organizations with a history of serving children, youth, women, and families living with HIV and operating in the area;section 300ff–71 of this title

children.label: L
children.text: grantees under other Federal HIV programs, including but not limited to providers of HIV prevention services; and

children.label: M
children.text: representatives of individuals who formerly were Federal, State, or local prisoners, were released from the custody of the penal system during the preceding 3 years, and had HIV/AIDS as of the date on which the individuals were so released.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In providing for a council for purposes of paragraph (1), a chief elected official receiving a grant under may establish the council directly or designate an existing entity to serve as the council, subject to subparagraph (B).section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.heading: Consideration regarding designation of council
children.label: B Consideration regarding designation of council
children.text: In making a determination of whether to establish or designate a council under subparagraph (A), a chief elected official receiving a grant under shall give priority to the designation of an existing entity that has demonstrated experience in planning for the HIV health care service needs within the eligible area and in the implementation of such plans in addressing those needs. Any existing entity so designated shall be expanded to include a broad representation of the full range of entities that provide such services within the geographic area to be served.section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: determine the size and demographics of the population of individuals with HIV/AIDS, as well as the size and demographics of the estimated population of individuals with HIV/AIDS who are unaware of their HIV status;

children.label: i
children.text: individuals with HIV/AIDS who know their HIV status and are not receiving HIV-related services;

children.label: ii
children.text: disparities in access and services among affected subpopulations and historically underserved communities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: individuals with HIV/AIDS who do not know their HIV status;

children.label: i
children.text: size and demographics of the population of individuals with HIV/AIDS (as determined under subparagraph (A)) and the needs of such population (as determined under subparagraph (B));

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstrated (or probable) cost effectiveness and outcome effectiveness of proposed strategies and interventions, to the extent that data are reasonably available;

children.label: iii
children.text: priorities of the communities with HIV/AIDS for whom the services are intended;

children.label: iv
children.text: coordination in the provision of services to such individuals with programs for HIV prevention and for the prevention and treatment of substance abuse, including programs that provide comprehensive treatment for such abuse;

children.label: v
children.text: availability of other governmental and non-governmental resources, including the State medicaid plan under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] and the State Children’s Health Insurance Program under title XXI of such Act [ et seq.] to cover health care costs of eligible individuals and families with HIV/AIDS; and42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1397aa

children.label: vi
children.text: capacity development needs resulting from disparities in the availability of HIV-related services in historically underserved communities;

children.label: i
children.text: includes a strategy for identifying individuals who know their HIV status and are not receiving such services and for informing the individuals of and enabling the individuals to utilize the services, giving particular attention to eliminating disparities in access and services among affected subpopulations and historically underserved communities, and including discrete goals, a timetable, and an appropriate allocation of funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: includes a strategy to coordinate the provision of such services with programs for HIV prevention (including outreach and early intervention) and for the prevention and treatment of substance abuse (including programs that provide comprehensive treatment services for such abuse);

children.label: iii
children.text: is compatible with any State or local plan for the provision of services to individuals with HIV/AIDS; and

children.label: iv
children.text: includes a strategy, coordinated as appropriate with other community strategies and efforts, including discrete goals, a timetable, and appropriate funding, for identifying individuals with HIV/AIDS who do not know their HIV status, making such individuals aware of such status, and enabling such individuals to use the health and support services described in , with particular attention to reducing barriers to routine testing and disparities in access and services among affected subpopulations and historically underserved communities;section 300ff–14 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: assess the efficiency of the administrative mechanism in rapidly allocating funds to the areas of greatest need within the eligible area, and at the discretion of the planning council, assess the effectiveness, either directly or through contractual arrangements, of the services offered in meeting the identified needs;

children.label: F
children.text: participate in the development of the statewide coordinated statement of need initiated by the State public health agency responsible for administering grants under part B;

children.label: G
children.text: establish methods for obtaining input on community needs and priorities which may include public meetings (in accordance with paragraph (7)), conducting focus groups, and convening ad-hoc panels; and

children.label: H
children.text: coordinate with Federal grantees that provide HIV-related services within the eligible area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The planning council under paragraph (1) may not be directly involved in the administration of a grant under . With respect to compliance with the preceding sentence, the planning council may not designate (or otherwise be involved in the selection of) particular entities as recipients of any of the amounts provided in the grant.section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.heading: Required agreements
children.label: B Required agreements
children.text: An individual may serve on the planning council under paragraph (1) only if the individual agrees that if the individual has a financial interest in an entity, if the individual is an employee of a public or private entity, or if the individual is a member of a public or private organization, and such entity or organization is seeking amounts from a grant under , the individual will not, with respect to the purpose for which the entity seeks such amounts, participate (directly or in an advisory capacity) in the process of selecting entities to receive such amounts for such purpose.section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: Not less than 33 percent of the council shall be individuals who are receiving HIV-related services pursuant to a grant under , are not officers, employees, or consultants to any entity that receives amounts from such a grant, and do not represent any such entity, and reflect the demographics of the population of individuals with HIV/AIDS as determined under paragraph (4)(A). For purposes of the preceding sentence, an individual shall be considered to be receiving such services if the individual is a parent of, or a caregiver for, a minor child who is receiving such services.section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: With respect to membership on the planning council, clause (i) may not be construed as having any effect on entities that receive funds from grants under any of parts B through F but do not receive funds from grants under , on officers or employees of such entities, or on individuals who represent such entities.section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.heading: Grievance procedures
children.label: 6 Grievance procedures
children.text: A planning council under paragraph (1) shall develop procedures for addressing grievances with respect to funding under this subpart, including procedures for submitting grievances that cannot be resolved to binding arbitration. Such procedures shall be described in the by-laws of the planning council and be consistent with the requirements of subsection (c).

children.label: A
children.text: The council may not be chaired solely by an employee of the grantee under .section 300ff–11(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: The meetings of the council shall be open to the public and shall be held only after adequate notice to the public.

children.label: ii
children.text: The records, reports, transcripts, minutes, agenda, or other documents which were made available to or prepared for or by the council shall be available for public inspection and copying at a single location.

children.label: iii
children.text: Detailed minutes of each meeting of the council shall be kept. The accuracy of all minutes shall be certified to by the chair of the council.

children.label: iv
children.text: This subparagraph does not apply to any disclosure of information of a personal nature that would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, including any disclosure of medical information or personnel matters.

children.heading: Models
children.label: A Models
children.text: The Secretary shall, through a process that includes consultations with grantees under this subpart and public and private experts in grievance procedures, arbitration, and mediation, develop model grievance procedures that may be implemented by the planning council under subsection (b)(1) and grantees under this subpart. Such model procedures shall describe the elements that must be addressed in establishing local grievance procedures and provide grantees with flexibility in the design of such local procedures.

children.heading: Review
children.label: B Review
children.text: The Secretary shall review grievance procedures established by the planning council and grantees under this subpart to determine if such procedures are adequate. In making such a determination, the Secretary shall assess whether such procedures permit legitimate grievances to be filed, evaluated, and resolved at the local level.

children.heading: Grantees
children.label: 2 Grantees
children.text: To be eligible to receive funds under this subpart, a grantee shall develop grievance procedures that are determined by the Secretary to be consistent with the model procedures developed under paragraph (1)(A). Such procedures shall include a process for submitting grievances to binding arbitration.

children.heading: Process for establishing allocation priorities
children.label: d Process for establishing allocation priorities

children.text: for establishing the number of individuals living with HIV/AIDS who are not receiving HIV-related health services; and
for carrying out the duties under subsection (b)(4) and .section 300ff–27(b) of this title

children.heading: Training guidance and materials
children.label: e Training guidance and materials
children.text: The Secretary shall provide to each chief elected official receiving a grant under guidelines and materials for training members of the planning council under paragraph (1) regarding the duties of the council.section 300ff–11(a) of this title
